name: Back Pain Relief
description: Comprehensive treatment for acute and chronic lower back pain using evidence-based manual therapy and exercise interventions.
overview: Our back pain relief program is designed for individuals suffering from mechanical or disc-related lower back pain. Through a combination of spinal mobilization, core strengthening, and postural retraining, patients typically experience significant pain reduction and improved functional capacity within 4-8 weeks.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Thorough evaluation of spine mobility, muscle strength, and pain patterns

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Customized plan based on McKenzie or movement-based classification

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Spinal mobilization and soft tissue release techniques

steps.title: Exercise Prescription
steps.description: Progressive core stabilization and flexibility exercises

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program and ergonomic education

name: Frozen Shoulder
description: Specialized rehabilitation for adhesive capsulitis to restore shoulder range of motion and function.
overview: Frozen shoulder treatment targets the stiff, painful capsule of the shoulder joint. Our program combines gentle joint mobilizations, stretching, and progressive strengthening to break the cycle of pain and stiffness. Most patients achieve significant improvement in mobility over 8-16 weeks.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Evaluation of active and passive shoulder range of motion

steps.title: Pain Management
steps.description: Modalities and gentle techniques to reduce inflammation

steps.title: Joint Mobilization
steps.description: Progressive capsular stretching and manual therapy

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Pendulum exercises and progressive stretching program

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program for sustained mobility

name: Arthritis Care
description: Holistic management of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis through joint-friendly exercise and pain relief techniques.
overview: Our arthritis care program helps individuals manage joint pain and maintain mobility despite degenerative changes. Treatment focuses on low-impact strengthening, joint protection strategies, and pain modulation. Patients learn to stay active while minimizing flare-ups.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Joint function evaluation and pain level assessment

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Individualized program based on affected joints

steps.title: Pain Management
steps.description: Heat therapy and gentle manual techniques

name: Sciatica Treatment
description: Targeted therapy for sciatic nerve pain caused by disc herniation or spinal stenosis.
overview: Sciatica treatment addresses the radiating leg pain caused by nerve compression. We use a combination of nerve gliding exercises, lumbar mobilization, and core stabilization to reduce irritation and promote healing. Most patients experience significant relief within 6-12 weeks.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Neurological screening and nerve tension testing

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Lumbar mobilization and nerve flossing techniques

name: Joint Rehabilitation
description: Comprehensive rehabilitation for joint injuries, stiffness, and post-injury recovery.
overview: Joint rehabilitation addresses a wide range of joint conditions from post-traumatic stiffness to degenerative changes. Our program restores range of motion, strength, and proprioception through progressive exercise and manual therapy. Suitable for any major joint of the body.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Joint-specific range of motion and strength testing

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Joint mobilization and soft tissue release

name: Cervical Spondylosis
description: Specialized care for age-related neck degeneration and associated pain and stiffness.
overview: Cervical spondylosis treatment addresses the wear-and-tear changes in the neck that cause pain, stiffness, and sometimes arm symptoms. We focus on improving neck mobility, strengthening deep neck muscles, and correcting posture to reduce mechanical stress on the cervical spine.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Cervical range of motion and neurological screening

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Individualized approach based on symptom pattern

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Cervical mobilization and soft tissue techniques

steps.title: Exercise Prescription
steps.description: Deep neck flexor strengthening and postural exercises

name: Disc Herniation
description: Conservative management of lumbar or cervical disc herniation without surgery.
overview: Our disc herniation program offers evidence-based conservative care for bulging or herniated discs. Through directional preference exercises, spinal stabilization, and progressive loading, we help the majority of patients avoid surgery and return to normal activities.

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Spinal mobilization to reduce disc pressure

name: Postural Correction
description: Program to correct poor posture habits and reduce associated pain and dysfunction.
overview: Postural correction addresses the forward head, rounded shoulders, and kyphotic postures common in desk workers. We use a combination of stretching tight structures, strengthening weak muscles, and postural awareness training to create lasting change.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Postural analysis and muscle imbalance evaluation

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Release of pectorals, upper traps, and suboccipitals

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Scapular stabilizer and deep neck flexor exercises

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
description: Conservative treatment for median nerve compression in the wrist.
overview: Carpal tunnel treatment focuses on reducing pressure on the median nerve through nerve gliding exercises, wrist splinting advice, and activity modification. We also address contributing factors like posture and repetitive strain to prevent recurrence.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Phalen's test, Tinel's sign, and sensory evaluation

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Carpal tunnel mobilization and nerve gliding

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Ergonomic workstation setup and activity pacing

name: Stroke Recovery
description: Comprehensive rehabilitation for stroke survivors to restore mobility, balance, and independence.
overview: Our stroke recovery program addresses hemiplegia, balance deficits, and functional limitations through task-specific training and neuroplasticity-based approaches. We focus on relearning movement patterns and maximizing independence in daily activities.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Motor function, balance, and functional capacity evaluation

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Goal-oriented rehabilitation based on stroke stage

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home program and community reintegration support

name: Parkinson's Therapy
description: Specialized exercise program to maintain mobility and reduce Parkinson's-related symptoms.
overview: Parkinson's therapy uses high-amplitude, big movement exercises to combat bradykinesia and rigidity. Our program incorporates cueing strategies, balance training, and functional tasks to improve quality of life and delay disease progression.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Motor symptoms, balance, and fall risk evaluation

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Individualized program based on disease stage

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: LSVT BIG or similar amplitude-based exercises

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program and caregiver education

name: Spinal Cord Rehab
description: Comprehensive rehabilitation for spinal cord injury to maximize function and independence.
overview: Spinal cord rehabilitation addresses paralysis, spasticity, and functional limitations through intensive task-specific training. Our program focuses on wheelchair skills, transfers, and activities of daily living to promote maximum independence.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: ASIA impairment scale and functional level evaluation

name: Bell's Palsy Treatment
description: Facial rehabilitation for Bell's palsy to restore muscle function and symmetry.
overview: Bell's palsy treatment addresses facial nerve paralysis through gentle facial exercises, electrical stimulation, and mirror feedback. Early intervention improves outcomes, with most patients achieving significant recovery within 3-6 months.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Facial muscle strength and symmetry evaluation

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Facial muscle exercises and mirror therapy

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program and eye care

name: Neuropathy Care
description: Management of peripheral neuropathy symptoms including pain, numbness, and balance issues.
overview: Neuropathy care addresses the sensory and motor symptoms of peripheral nerve damage. We focus on balance training to prevent falls, pain management strategies, and foot care education. Treatment helps patients maintain mobility despite sensory loss.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Sensory testing, balance, and fall risk evaluation

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Individualized balance and pain management protocol

steps.title: Balance Training
steps.description: Progressive balance exercises and fall prevention

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Foot care education and home safety

name: Multiple Sclerosis Rehab
description: Exercise and rehabilitation for MS patients to manage fatigue and maintain function.
overview: Multiple sclerosis rehabilitation addresses fatigue, weakness, spasticity, and balance issues. Our program uses energy conservation techniques, graded exercise, and cooling strategies to help patients stay active without exacerbating symptoms.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Fatigue levels, mobility, and symptom evaluation

name: Vestibular Rehabilitation
description: Specialized therapy for dizziness, vertigo, and balance disorders of vestibular origin.
overview: Vestibular rehabilitation addresses benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV), vestibular hypofunction, and other balance disorders. We use canalith repositioning maneuvers, habituation exercises, and gaze stabilization to reduce dizziness and improve balance.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Dix-Hallpike test and vestibular function evaluation

steps.title: Canalith Repositioning
steps.description: Epley or Semont maneuver for BPPV

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program and fall prevention

name: ACL Recovery Program
description: Structured rehabilitation following ACL reconstruction or conservative management.
overview: Our ACL recovery program follows evidence-based phases from early protection through return to sport. We focus on restoring knee stability, quadriceps strength, and neuromuscular control. Athletes typically return to sport within 6-12 months post-surgery.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Knee stability, range of motion, and strength evaluation

name: Tennis Elbow Treatment
description: Effective treatment for lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow) pain and dysfunction.
overview: Tennis elbow treatment addresses the overuse tendinopathy of the wrist extensors. We use a combination of eccentric strengthening, manual therapy, and activity modification. Most patients achieve pain-free function within 6-12 weeks.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Pain provocation and grip strength testing

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Load management and progressive loading protocol

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Gradual return to activity and prevention

name: Runner's Knee Treatment
description: Rehabilitation for patellofemoral pain syndrome common in runners.
overview: Runner's knee treatment addresses patellofemoral pain through hip and knee strengthening, patellar taping, and running biomechanics retraining. We identify and correct contributing factors like weak glutes and overpronation.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Patellar tracking, hip strength, and running analysis

name: Ankle Sprain Rehab
description: Progressive rehabilitation for acute and chronic ankle sprains.
overview: Ankle sprain rehab restores stability and prevents recurrence through progressive strengthening, balance training, and proprioception work. We address both acute injuries and chronic instability from repeated sprains.

name: Rotator Cuff Injury
description: Rehabilitation for rotator cuff tears and tendinopathy.
overview: Rotator cuff treatment addresses tears and tendinopathy through progressive strengthening, scapular stabilization, and activity modification. Many patients achieve excellent outcomes with conservative care; we also prepare those who need surgery for optimal recovery.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Strength testing, impingement signs, and imaging review

steps.title: Manual Therapy
steps.description: Soft tissue release and joint mobilizations

name: Hamstring Tear Recovery
description: Structured rehabilitation for hamstring muscle strains and tears.
overview: Hamstring tear recovery uses progressive loading and Nordic curl exercises to reduce reinjury risk. We address the high recurrence rate through adequate healing time and eccentric strengthening before return to sprinting.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Pain level, strength, and flexibility evaluation

name: Shin Splints Treatment
description: Treatment for medial tibial stress syndrome (shin splints) in runners.
overview: Shin splints treatment addresses the overuse injury common in runners and military personnel. We focus on load management, calf stretching, and strengthening, plus running biomechanics to address contributing factors.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Pain location, calf flexibility, and running analysis

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Load reduction and progressive loading protocol

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Calf strengthening and foot intrinsic exercises

name: Sports Massage Therapy
description: Therapeutic massage for athletes to enhance recovery and performance.
overview: Sports massage addresses muscle tension, adhesions, and recovery between training sessions. We use deep tissue, trigger point, and stretching techniques to improve flexibility, reduce soreness, and prevent injury.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Muscle tension and problem area evaluation

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Targeted approach based on sport and goals

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Deep tissue and trigger point release

name: Balance Training
description: Progressive balance exercises to improve stability and reduce fall risk.
overview: Balance training is essential for older adults to maintain independence and prevent falls. Our program uses evidence-based exercises including Tai Chi elements, single-leg stance, and perturbation training to improve postural control.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Balance testing and fall risk screening

steps.title: Active Treatment
steps.description: Progressive static and dynamic balance exercises

steps.title: Dual-Task Training
steps.description: Balance with cognitive or motor dual tasks

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Home exercise program and class options

name: Fall Prevention Program
description: Comprehensive program to reduce fall risk in older adults.
overview: Our fall prevention program combines balance training, strength work, home safety assessment, and medication review coordination. We address the multiple risk factors that contribute to falls in older adults.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Fall history, balance, strength, and home safety

name: Osteoporosis Management
description: Safe exercise program for bone health and fracture prevention.
overview: Osteoporosis management uses weight-bearing and resistance exercises to maintain or improve bone density. Our program is designed with safety in mind, avoiding high-impact or flexion activities that could cause vertebral fracture.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Bone density review, fracture risk, and posture

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.description: Safe exercise protocol for bone health

name: Mobility Improvement
description: Program to restore and maintain functional mobility in older adults.
overview: Mobility rehabilitation addresses the decline in movement ability that often accompanies aging. We focus on joint flexibility, functional strength, and task-specific practice to help patients maintain independence in daily activities.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Functional mobility and activity limitation evaluation

name: Strength Building
description: Age-appropriate strength training to combat sarcopenia and maintain function.
overview: Strength building for older adults uses progressive resistance training to combat muscle loss and maintain independence. Our program is tailored to each individual's baseline, with emphasis on functional movements and safe progression.

name: Joint Stiffness Relief
description: Treatment for age-related joint stiffness and reduced range of motion.
overview: Joint stiffness relief addresses the morning stiffness and reduced mobility common in older adults. We use gentle mobilizations, stretching, and heat therapy to improve joint mobility and reduce discomfort.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Joint range of motion and stiffness pattern

name: Geriatric Arthritis
description: Specialized arthritis care tailored for older adults with multiple joint involvement.
overview: Geriatric arthritis care addresses the unique needs of older adults with osteoarthritis, including pain management, joint protection, and maintaining independence. We consider comorbidities and polypharmacy in our approach.

name: Gait Training
description: Walking retraining to improve walking pattern, speed, and safety.
overview: Gait training addresses abnormal walking patterns, slow gait speed, and fear of walking. We use treadmill training, overground practice, and cueing strategies to improve walking efficiency and confidence.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Gait analysis and walking speed measurement

steps.title: Obstacle Training
steps.description: Stepping over obstacles and uneven surfaces

name: Knee Replacement Rehab
description: Comprehensive rehabilitation following total knee arthroplasty.
overview: Knee replacement rehab restores range of motion, strength, and function following TKA. Our program follows evidence-based protocols from early mobilization through return to activities. Most patients achieve excellent outcomes within 12 weeks.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: ROM, swelling, and surgical protocol review

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Return to activities and long-term care

name: Hip Replacement Rehab
description: Rehabilitation following total hip arthroplasty for optimal recovery.
overview: Hip replacement rehab addresses the specific precautions (posterior vs anterior approach) while restoring mobility and strength. We focus on safe progression to walking, stairs, and return to daily activities.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Surgical approach, precautions, and baseline function

name: Spinal Surgery Recovery
description: Rehabilitation following spinal fusion, discectomy, or other spine surgery.
overview: Spinal surgery recovery follows surgeon-specific protocols for fusion, laminectomy, or discectomy. We progress from early mobilization through core strengthening while respecting lifting and movement restrictions.

steps.title: Early Phase
steps.description: Safe mobility and gentle core activation

name: Shoulder Surgery Recovery
description: Rehabilitation following rotator cuff repair, labral repair, or other shoulder surgery.
overview: Shoulder surgery recovery follows protocol-specific timelines for rotator cuff repair, labral repair, or total shoulder replacement. We balance protection with progressive mobilization and strengthening.

name: Ligament Repair Rehab
description: Rehabilitation following ACL, MCL, or other ligament repair surgery.
overview: Ligament repair rehab follows evidence-based protocols for knee, ankle, or other ligament reconstructions. We progress through protection, strengthening, and return-to-sport phases with appropriate milestones.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Surgical procedure and graft type review

steps.title: Recovery & Maintenance
steps.description: Return to sport testing and clearance

name: Fracture Rehabilitation
description: Rehabilitation following bone fracture and cast or surgical fixation.
overview: Fracture rehabilitation addresses the stiffness, weakness, and functional loss that follow immobilization. We restore range of motion, strength, and function through progressive exercise once healing allows.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Healing status, ROM, and strength evaluation

name: Arthroscopy Recovery
description: Rehabilitation following knee, shoulder, or other joint arthroscopy.
overview: Arthroscopy recovery addresses the minimally invasive procedures for meniscus repair, labral debridement, or cartilage procedures. Recovery is typically faster than open surgery but still requires structured rehabilitation.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Procedure type and surgeon protocol review

name: Joint Fusion Recovery
description: Rehabilitation following spinal or peripheral joint fusion surgery.
overview: Joint fusion recovery addresses the unique rehabilitation needs after arthrodesis. We focus on protecting the fusion while mobilizing adjacent joints and restoring functional strength. Spinal fusion requires particular attention to movement restrictions.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Fusion site, protocol, and adjacent joint status
